description_long: This is a robust pair of bolt cutters featuring long, straight handles with black grips and a heavy-duty, angular cutting head. The jaws are broad and triangular, designed for cutting through thick metal rods or bolts, and are joined by a prominent central pivot. The handles are dark, likely made of metal with a protective coating, and end in textured grips for secure handling. The cutting head is metallic, with a matte, slightly rough finish, and the pivot mechanism is reinforced with visible bolts. The overall construction suggests durability and strength, suitable for demanding urban tasks such as cutting locks, chains, or fencing. No visible branding or logos are present on the tool.
description: A heavy-duty pair of bolt cutters with long, dark handles and a broad, angular cutting head, designed for cutting metal objects. The tool features textured black grips and a reinforced central pivot.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full length of the bolt cutters, with both handles and the wide, triangular jaws clearly visible.
description_view_1: Side view revealing the thin profile of the jaws and handles, emphasizing the flatness and alignment of the tool.
description_view_2: Rear view, nearly identical to the front, displaying the handles and the back of the cutting head.
description_view_3: Opposite side view, again showing the slim profile and the straight alignment of the handles and jaws.
attribute_handle_type: Straight, cylindrical handles with black rubberized grips for comfort and slip resistance.
attribute_jaw_design: Broad, angular, flat-edged jaws designed for heavy-duty cutting, typical of bolt cutters, with no serrations or wire cutter notches.
attribute_pivot_mechanism: Fixed central pivot with visible bolts, providing strong leverage but no adjustable jaw width.
attribute_grip_pattern: Handles feature a smooth, rubberized grip with subtle texturing for enhanced hold.
